title: lepisanthes dictyophylla
intro: Lepisanthes dictyophylla, also known as the "Makabuhay" or "Tree of Life," is a species of flowering plant in the family Salicaceae. It is a small to medium-sized tree native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, and its bark, leaves, and roots have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which are said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating diarrhea, and alleviating respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of Lepisanthes dictyophylla are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, and are also used in traditional folk medicine to treat various ailments.
